body_html:
Daphney and Frankie,

I took a closer look at your Zola inquiry and want to send you something more useful than the menu PDFs you've already gotten from us.

$8,000 for 130 guests at Branford House is tight but doable — and the way you've structured your planning (venue locked, DJ booked, real budget allocated) tells me you didn't pick that number by accident. I'm going to respect that.

Two real options at your headcount:

Option A — Buffet, $8,000 ceiling
Full buffet (two mains, three sides, salad), passed appetizers during cocktail hour, full-service staff for setup and breakdown. Real plates and silverware included. Travel to Groton built in. This works at your number.

Option B — Buffet + Carving Station, ~$9,500
Same as A, plus a live churrasco carving station during dinner — the experience that makes guests remember the food a year later. $1,500 over your cap, but for 130 guests that's about $11.50/person more.

I can put together a clean side-by-side so you and Frankie can see exactly what each one looks like. Or, if it's easier, I can hop on a quick 15-20 minute call this week and walk through it.

What works better for you?

Andre
Comeketo Catering
